name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Honolulu, Hi
description_short: NACA offers character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and below-market fixed rates. A non-profit that has helped 500K+ homeowners despite credit challenges.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years working to close the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership. The organization has evolved from negotiating the first-ever housing trust fund into a major force in combating predatory lending practices and corporate real estate speculation.

NACA offers character-based mortgage lending that ignores traditional credit scores, instead evaluating borrowers on their overall financial circumstances and ability to repay. Their flagship mortgage product features no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and fixed interest rates below current market rates. As of April 2026, they offer rates as low as 5% for 15-year mortgages, 5.125% for 20-year terms, and 5.625% for 30-year mortgages. The organization also provides HUD housing counseling, serves as a platform for rental rights advocacy against predatory landlords, and operates "Achieve the Dream" events across the country to help members become NACA-qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is its mission-driven, non-profit structure explicitly focused on equity and racial wealth gap closure rather than profit maximization. They've served 3 million people overall, helped 500K homeowners achieve ownership, and have $20B in mortgage commitments. The organization actively campaigns against corporate landlords and Wall Street real estate investors, positioning itself as an advocacy organization alongside a lender. Their character-based underwriting explicitly removes credit score barriers that traditionally exclude low-income and communities of color from homeownership.

However, potential borrowers should understand that NACA requires active participation—members must attend multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events and engage in the organization's mission-driven community work. The mortgage qualification process appears to involve more steps than conventional lenders. While their rates are competitive, they may not always beat every refinance opportunity depending on market conditions and individual circumstances. Geographic reach appears concentrated around event locations rather than nationwide accessibility.
pros: No down payment required—removes primary barrier to homeownership for low-income borrowers
No closing costs or fees—saves thousands compared to traditional mortgage lenders
No mortgage insurance—eliminates PMI costs that typically burden low-down-payment borrowers
Credit score not considered—character-based underwriting includes borrowers rejected by conventional lenders
Below-market fixed rates (5% to 5.625% as of April 2026)—competitive pricing for qualified members
Served 500K+ homeowners and maintains $20B in mortgage commitments—proven track record and scale
HUD housing counseling and financial education included—30% of all HUD counseling provided by NACA
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events—membership is not purely online/remote, demands time investment
Geographic limitations—events only in select cities (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis, etc.), not nationwide accessibility
Less transparent pricing on website—specific rate details and qualification criteria not fully detailed; requires member portal access
Mission-driven participation expected—implicit expectation of involvement in advocacy and community work, not purely transactional lending
Limited information about processing timelines and approval rates—website focuses on mission and testimonials rather than operational metrics
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers with credit challenges or no credit history seeking first-time homeownership
Communities of color and marginalized populations seeking to build generational wealth through homeownership
Borrowers in NACA event cities willing to invest time in financial education and community engagement
Homebuyers who value mission-aligned lending and advocacy against predatory real estate practices
diagnosis: NACA is best for credit-challenged, low-income, and first-time homebuyers—particularly in communities of color—who can attend in-person events and align with the organization's anti-predatory lending mission. The main caveat is that this is not a traditional lender: membership requires participation in multi-day events and community engagement, and geographic reach is limited to event cities, making it inaccessible for remote or geographically distant borrowers.
services: Character-based mortgage lending without credit score consideration
No down payment mortgages with fixed rates
No closing costs or fees mortgages
Mortgage rate locks and fixed-rate financing (15, 20, and 30-year terms)
HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of national HUD counseling)
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' financial education events
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
Predatory landlord advocacy and rental rights support
Partner network (lenders, servicers, real estate agents, home inspectors)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
